sets.title: A New Hobby
paragraphs: Last year I decided to (1) ___ up photography as a hobby. I borrowed my dad's old camera and started taking photos (2) ___ my neighbourhood. At first my photos were not very good, but I kept practising.
I watched videos online (3) ___ taught me about lighting and composition. Slowly my pictures began to improve. Now I enter (4) ___ school competitions and have already won one prize.
Photography has helped me see the world (5) ___ differently. I notice small details (6) ___ I never noticed before, like the way light reflects on a puddle or how shadows fall on walls.

gaps.rationale: 'Take up' is the fixed phrasal verb meaning to start a new hobby.
'Around my neighbourhood' or 'in my neighbourhood' are both correct.
Relative pronoun referring to the videos — 'that' or 'which'.
'Enter some competitions' — 'some' is the natural quantifier here.
'See the world so/completely differently' expresses a change in perspective.
Relative clause referring to details — 'that' or 'which'.

sets.title: My Best Friend
paragraphs: I have known my best friend Sara (1) ___ we were seven years old. We met at primary school and quickly became friends because (2) ___ of us liked the same things: books, music and animals.
We do not go to (3) ___ same school any more because her family moved to another part of the city. However, we still see each other (4) ___ the weekend and chat online almost every day.
I think a good friendship is (5) ___ on trust and honesty. Sara always tells me the truth, even when I do not want to hear it. That is (6) ___ I think she is my best friend.

gaps.rationale: 'Known her since we were seven' — 'since' introduces the starting point.
'Both of us liked the same things' — 'both' refers to the two friends.
Definite article before 'same school'.
'See each other at the weekend' — 'at' is used with 'weekend' in British English.
'Based on trust and honesty' — the correct participle adjective.
'That is why I think she is my best friend' — reason connector.

sets.title: My School Day
paragraphs: I usually wake (1) ___ at half past six and get ready for school. I have breakfast (2) ___ my family before leaving the house at quarter past seven.
School starts at eight o'clock. (3) ___ the morning we have four lessons. We have a twenty-minute break at ten thirty and then two more lessons before lunch.

I eat in the school canteen with my friends. In (4) ___ afternoon we have two more lessons and then we finish at four o'clock. After school I go (5) ___ to the library to do my homework. I try to finish (6) ___ before dinner so I have time to relax in the evening.

gaps.rationale: 'Wake up' — fixed phrasal verb meaning to stop sleeping.
'Have breakfast with my family' — 'with' for shared activity.
'In the morning' — standard time expression.
'In the afternoon' — definite article before 'afternoon'.
'Go straight to the library' or 'go back to the library'.
'Finish it before dinner' — 'it' refers to the homework.

sets.title: The School Play
paragraphs: Every year our school puts (1) ___ a play for parents and students. This year we are performing a comedy, which is (2) ___ harder to act than a serious drama because the timing of each joke must be perfect.

I am playing one (3) ___ the main characters. I have been learning my lines for (4) ___ months and I still make mistakes sometimes. My drama teacher says the most important thing is not to stop (5) ___ you forget a word — just keep going and the audience will not notice.
The performance is next Friday. I am a (6) ___ nervous but also very excited about it.

gaps.rationale: 'Put on a play' — fixed phrasal verb meaning to organise a performance.
'Much harder' or 'a lot harder' — comparative with uncountable degree.
'One of the main characters' — partitive structure.
Any plural count indicating several months of practice.
'Don't stop if/when you forget a word' — conditional or temporal.
'A little nervous' or 'a bit nervous' — both are natural hedges.

sets.title: Healthy Eating at School
paragraphs: Our school has recently (1) ___ changes to its canteen menu. There are (2) ___ more vegetables, salads and fruit available, and less fried food.
Some students were not happy (3) ___ first because they liked the old menu. However, most people now agree that the food tastes better (4) ___ it is fresher.

The school also runs a programme to teach students (5) ___ to cook simple healthy meals. Students learn how to prepare dishes using ingredients (6) ___ are in season, which means they are usually cheaper and more nutritious.

gaps.rationale: 'Made changes' — 'make changes' collocates; past participle after 'has'.
'Now more vegetables' or 'many more vegetables' — quantity or recency.
'Not happy at first' — 'at first' is a fixed adverbial phrase.
Causal connectors — 'because/as/since it is fresher'.
'Teach students how to cook' — indirect question with 'how'.
Relative pronoun for 'ingredients that/which are in season'.

sets.title: Learning a Language
paragraphs: I have been learning French (1) ___ three years. I started because I wanted to speak to my grandmother, (2) ___ moved to France when she was young. She is very pleased that I am learning her language.
At school we do reading, writing and listening exercises. (3) ___ my opinion, speaking is the most difficult skill because you have to think quickly and there is no time to check a dictionary.
I try to practise as (4) ___ as possible by watching French films and listening (5) ___ French music. I also chat online (6) ___ a French pen pal I found through a language exchange website.

gaps.rationale: 'Learning French for three years' — 'for' expresses duration.
Relative pronoun referring to grandmother — subject of the clause.
'In my opinion' — fixed expression.
'As much as possible' or 'as often as possible' — both natural here.
'Listening to French music' — 'listen to' is the fixed phrasal verb.
'Chat with a pen pal' — 'with' for interacting with someone.

sets.title: Weekend in the Mountains
paragraphs: Last weekend my family and I went (1) ___ a camping trip in the mountains. We drove for about two hours (2) ___ we arrived at the campsite.

We set (3) ___ our tent and cooked dinner over an open fire. It was much colder than I expected, (4) ___ we had sleeping bags and warm clothes. In the morning we went on a long walk through the forest.
I am not usually (5) ___ of outdoor activities, but this trip was really special. I am already (6) ___ forward to the next one.

gaps.rationale: 'Go on a camping trip' — 'on' is fixed with 'trip'.
'Drove for two hours before/until we arrived' — temporal connector.
'Set up a tent' — fixed phrasal verb.
'Colder than expected, but we had sleeping bags' — contrast connector.
'Keen on outdoor activities' or 'fond of outdoor activities'.
'Looking forward to the next one' — fixed phrase with 'look forward to'.

sets.title: Being a Good Student
paragraphs: Being a good student does not (1) ___ mean getting top marks in every test. It also means being curious, asking questions and (2) ___ your best even when something is difficult.
Good students usually have a regular study routine and make (3) ___ of class time. They also ask for help (4) ___ they do not understand something rather than pretending they do.

It is important to look (5) ___ your physical and mental health too. If you are tired or stressed, it is hard to concentrate. Getting enough sleep and taking breaks (6) ___ study helps your brain work better.

gaps.rationale: 'Does not just mean' — 'just' narrows the meaning.
'Doing your best' or 'trying your best' — gerund as subject complement.
'Make use of class time' — fixed phrasal noun.
'Ask for help when/if they don't understand' — conditional/temporal.
'Look after your health' — phrasal verb meaning take care of.
'Taking breaks from study' — 'from' indicates the activity paused.

sets.title: A Special Teacher
paragraphs: When I think (1) ___ the teachers who have influenced me most, I always think of my primary school English teacher, Ms Patel. She made every lesson (2) ___ interesting and fun.

Ms Patel was always patient and never made us (3) ___ embarrassed if we made a mistake. She believed that mistakes were part of learning. She encouraged us to read books that we enjoyed (4) ___ of books we had to read for school.
Because of her, I developed a love (5) ___ reading and writing that I still have today. I sometimes wonder (6) ___ would have happened if I had not had such a great teacher.

gaps.rationale: 'Think about' or 'think of' teachers — both correct.
'Made every lesson feel/seem interesting' — causative with adjective.
'Made us feel embarrassed' — causative construction.
'Instead of books we had to read' — fixed preposition phrase.
'A love of reading' or 'a love for reading' — both accepted.
'Wonder what would have happened' — embedded question with 'what'.

sets.title: Climate Change and Young People
paragraphs: Many young people (1) ___ the world are concerned about climate change. They want governments and companies to take action (2) ___ protect the environment for future generations.

Some teenagers show their concern by joining environmental groups or taking (3) ___ in protests. Others focus on making small changes in (4) ___ own lives, such as using less plastic, cycling instead of travelling by car, and buying second-hand clothes.

Experts say that young people's voices (5) ___ important because they will live with the consequences of climate change for longer than older people. Every action, (6) ___ small, can make a difference.

gaps.rationale: 'Around the world' or 'across the world' — both mean globally.
'Take action to protect' — infinitive of purpose.
'Taking part in protests' — 'take part in' is the fixed phrasal verb.
'In their own lives' — possessive pronoun referring back to teenagers.
'Voices are important' — plural subject needs plural verb.
'However small' or 'no matter how small' — concessive clause.
